Case title: Multi-agent scientific idea generation as a boundary case for social-science agenda work
Short description: Treat agent teams that brainstorm, critique, and refine ideas as a boundary case for whether collaborative AI can displace human agenda setting.
Empirical unit of analysis: Generated idea portfolios and reviewer scores
Key typology question: Do multi-agent gains in novelty imply anything about social-science relevance or mechanism quality?
Observable artifacts / traces: Idea portfolios, novelty metrics, reviewer ratings, comparison prompts
Key outcomes observed: Novelty, feasibility, diversity, alignment with research trends
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Multi-agent idea generation produces socially relevant and mechanism-rich agendas without human filtering
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Most evidence comes from non-social-science domains and model-internal evaluations
Why this case has high leverage: Helpful because it pressures the temptation to generalize from scientific ideation benchmarks to social-science role claims.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
